content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup. The CockroachDB cluster uses `crdb-cluster-certs` secret mounted at `/cockroach/cockroach-certs`. The secret was deleted by the adversary.

I need to:
1. Generate a new node key and cert valid for 365 days in the openssl-toolbox
2. Also need client.root key and cert
3. Create the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret with: ca.crt, node.crt, node.key, client.root.crt, client.root.key
4. Do a rolling restart of the pods to pick up the new secret

The pods are currently showing as Running even though the secret is gone—they might be using cached certs in memory or the deletion happened recently. Let me start by generating fresh certificates in the toolbox, beginning with a new node key and certificate signing request valid for a year, then signing it with the CA.

I also need to regenerate the client root cert since the existing one only has 2 days of validity left. Once I have all the new certificates, I'll create the secret and perform the rolling restart.

content.text: The `crdb-cluster-certs` secret is missing (deleted by the adversary). The pods are running but will fail to restart without it. I'll generate new 1-year certs and recreate the secret.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -noout -enddate -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt 2>&1
input.description: Check client.root cert expiry

content.text: Both the node cert (2 days) and client cert (2 days) need to be regenerated with ~1-year validity. Let me generate new certs in the toolbox:

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
# Generate new node key
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/node.key.new 2048 2>&1
echo "node key generated"
'
input.description: Generate new node private key

content.content: node key generated

tool_use_result.stdout: node key generated

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
# Generate CSR using existing node.cnf
openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/node.key.new \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.csr.new \
  -config /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1
echo "node CSR generated"
'
input.description: Generate new node CSR

content.content: node CSR generated

tool_use_result.stdout: node CSR generated

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
# Sign the new node cert with CA, valid for 365 days
openssl x509 -req \
  -in /tmp/certs/node.csr.new \
  -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt \
  -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.crt.new \
  -days 365 \
  -sha256 \
  -extfile /tmp/certs/node.cnf \
  -extensions v3_req 2>&1
echo "node cert signed"
'
input.description: Sign new node cert with CA for 365 days

content.thinking: Good, the new node cert is valid until Jul 1 2027 (1 year). Now I need to also generate a new client.root cert for the `root` user.

content.text: New node cert valid until Jul 1 2027. Now generate the client root cert:

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
# Generate new client.root key
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new 2048 2>&1

# Generate CSR for root client cert
openssl req -new \
  -key /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new \
  -subj "/CN=root" 2>&1

# Sign with CA for 365 days, with clientAuth extension
openssl x509 -req \
  -in /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new \
  -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt \
  -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new \
  -days 365 \
  -sha256 \
  -extfile <(printf "[v3_client]\nkeyUsage=critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment\nextendedKeyUsage=clientAuth") \
  -extensions v3_client 2>&1

echo "client.root cert done"
openssl x509 -noout -enddate -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new
openssl verify -CAfile /tmp/certs/ca.crt /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new
'
input.description: Generate and sign new client.root cert for 365 days

subject=CN = root
client.root cert done

content.text: Both certs are good. Now extract all the cert/key data and create the Kubernetes secret:

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
# Read all required files and base64 encode them
echo "=== ca.crt ==="
base64 /tmp/certs/ca.crt | tr -d "\n"
echo ""
echo "=== node.crt ==="
base64 /tmp/certs/node.crt.new | tr -d "\n"
echo ""
echo "=== node.key ==="
base64 /tmp/certs/node.key.new | tr -d "\n"
echo ""
echo "=== client.root.crt ==="
base64 /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new | tr -d "\n"
echo ""
echo "=== client.root.key ==="
base64 /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new | tr -d "\n"
echo ""
' 2>&1 > /tmp/cert_data.txt
echo "Done extracting cert data"
wc -l /tmp/cert_data.txt
input.description: Extract base64-encoded cert data from toolbox

content.content:
Done extracting cert data
10 /tmp/cert_data.txt

tool_use_result.stdout:
Done extracting cert data
10 /tmp/cert_data.txt

input.command:
CA_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- base64 /tmp/certs/ca.crt | tr -d '\n')
NODE_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- base64 /tmp/certs/node.crt.new | tr -d '\n')
NODE_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- base64 /tmp/certs/node.key.new | tr -d '\n')
CLIENT_ROOT_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- base64 /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new | tr -d '\n')
CLIENT_ROOT_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- base64 /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new | tr -d '\n')

kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-literal="ca.crt=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/ca.crt)" \
  --from-literal="node.crt=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new)" \
  --from-literal="node.key=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new)" \
  --from-literal="client.root.crt=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new)" \
  --from-literal="client.root.key=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new)" \
  2>&1
input.description: Create crdb-cluster-certs secret with new 1-year certs

input.command:
# Poll until rollout is complete
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset/crdb-cluster --timeout=5s 2>&1)
  echo "$STATUS"
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -q "successfully rolled out"; then
    break
  fi
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods 2>&1 | grep crdb-cluster
  sleep 5
done

content.thinking:
The pod is having liveness heartbeat failures - this is because it was just restarted with a new cert and is trying to re-join the cluster. CockroachDB needs quorum to function. The issue is that crdb-cluster-0 was restarted, has new certs, but is experiencing liveness issues because the other nodes (crdb-cluster-1, crdb-cluster-2) haven't been restarted yet and have the old certs (mounted from the now-deleted secret, but cached in the pod's ephemeral filesystem).

Wait - actually the secret was already deleted BEFORE I created it. 

When a secret gets deleted, pods that reference it via volumes keep running with whatever was cached in the mount point, but on restart they can't mount the missing secret anymore. Now that I've recreated the secret with new certs, restarting pods will pick up the new certificates. The liveness issues are likely temporary - CockroachDB should recover once the cluster stabilizes during the rolling restart.

Actually, looking at the StatefulSet configuration, it has `Parallel` pod management policy, which means all pods restart simultaneously rather than one at a time. That would cause a quorum loss temporarily, explaining the liveness probe failures. The logs show crdb-cluster-1 and crdb-cluster-2 are Ready now, so the cluster is recovering.

content.text: The cluster is stabilizing during the rolling restart. Let me wait for it to complete:

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset/crdb-cluster --timeout=180s 2>&1
input.description: Wait for rollout to fully complete

description: Wait for rollout to fully complete
